source: CT6-6 §12 (SEC) + §10 (BG) — the shared substrate for the service/server tier; user chose all components, in-repo separable, one Anthropic key, Ed25519 handshake

scenarios: a tampered or replayed submission is rejected
acceptance_criteria: stdlib-only pure-Python Ed25519 (RFC 8032); verify total on adversarial input; malleability S>=L rejected

scenarios: scheduler runs due tasks, self-checks, and emits boot/restart descriptors
acceptance_criteria: self-check flags stale tasks; failing task never crashes the loop
per-OS boot/restart install descriptors (systemd/launchd/Task Scheduler), injection-guarded

scenarios: the one key serves both roles and is never logged
acceptance_criteria: LLMClient adapter; real Anthropic call lazy; FakeLLMClient for tests

scenarios: the honest boundary + separability are documented
acceptance_criteria: README states design-not-deployed + separation plan + decided model
full suite green cp1252 AND PYTHONUTF8=1

testing_authorizations.playwright: N/A — no UI/frontend surface (a stdlib-only service substrate + tests + docs).
testing_authorizations.dev_api_integration: N/A — the live triage server / Anthropic API are external infra (not in-repo); the crypto + runtime core are exercised by the pytest suite + cross-validated by the security review vs libsodium + RFC 8032.

confirmed_stubs.why: the SEC-4 genuine-logger algorithm is the separable closed/paid piece; the open core ships a pluggable hook + a stdlib HMAC stub by design (documented), not a gap
the real Anthropic LLM call needs the SDK + network (the separated service tier's own dep, REPO-4); the stdlib core injects FakeLLMClient — an honest adapter boundary, not a gap
